frameworks.name: divisive normalization
frameworks.one_line: Feature-tuned divisive gain control as substrate enabling attentional modulation.

frameworks.name: attentional gain modulation
frameworks.one_line: Attentional response gain framed as consequence of normalization strength.

core_question: Does a visuocortical subpopulation's degree of divisive normalization determine its potential for attentional modulation?
key_assumptions: fMRI BOLD reflects population-level visuocortical activity
stimuli sharing features normalize one another more strongly
feature-tuned normalization can be quantified per subpopulation
manipulations: feature-overlap of paired stimuli (shared vs distinct features)
stimulus configuration forcing weaker vs stronger normalization regime
key_findings: responses to feature-sharing stimuli show stronger mutual normalization
subpopulations with stronger normalization show larger attentional benefits
forcing a subpopulation into stronger normalization increases its attentional gain
interpretations: normalization strength gates the magnitude of attentional modulation in visual cortex
